seed: tenderness vs rage; inside_vs_outside; quiet_channel; climb; constellation against rain; pixel_bleed; saturated_vs_muted; crown; guilt_forgiveness | abstract variation request 360; target batch count 500
title: Emotional Symbiosis
reasoning: The emotional tone encapsulates the duality of tenderness and rage. The color palette of saturated red, muted black, and soft pale blue resonates with the themes of intensity and calm. The rage emerges from the corner with sharp, jagged red pixels, colliding with the static and tender pale blue fields, reflecting a struggle between chaos and calm. This collision occurs along the diagonal, creating a tension that invites the viewer into an unspoken dialogue between the contrasting emotions. The occasional broken pixels serve as scarring, hinting at past conflicts; they enhance the complexity rather than distract, ensuring space for contemplation that resonates with the themes of guilt and forgiveness. Negative space is preserved for quiet reflection, while the flow disrupts expectations, inviting a closer look at the dynamism in stillness.

layout.name: corner_invasion
layout.description: One emotion starts in a corner and spreads diagonally.

composition.name: rise_vs_fall
composition.idea: Pixels move upward on one side and downward on the other.

referenceExample.title: Rage Entering Calm
referenceExample.pattern: red jagged pixels cutting into blue horizontal bands

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
